situation: Create /be-smarter: a universal skill that gives any model decision-kit's divergent-then-convergent thinking method on any task, with an optional human-in-the-loop dial. Ship it in a new repo modeled on decision-kit (Apache 2.0, same contribution method). Human-readable decision pages render only when a human is in the loop.

decisions.title: Core thinking protocol
decisions.question: What is the thinking protocol, exactly?

decisions.title: Depth calibration
decisions.question: How does the skill decide how much thinking a task deserves?

decisions.title: Human-in-the-loop dial
decisions.question: How does the human get into (and out of) the loop?

decisions.title: Thinking artifacts
decisions.question: What does the thinking leave behind?

options.C: Machine trace always, views on demand

decisions.title: Portability
decisions.question: What does 'improves any model's thinking' mean concretely?

options.D: Builder layer: system prompt + API recipe

decisions.title: Repo & positioning
decisions.question: Where does /be-smarter live, and what is it to decision-kit?

options.C: Umbrella org for a kit family

decisions.reasoning: Sibling repo. Constraints for implementation: write the repo in a tone similar to decision-kit, and do not use em-dashes anywhere in the repo prose.
